basics.name: Enea Manzi
basics.label: Cyber Security Researcher
basics.summary: Cyber Security graduate (M.Sc., University of Milan, 110/110 cum laude), currently applying for an intersectoral Ph.D. programme at the University of Milan. Research focus on API and cloud-native security, with hands-on experience in Kubernetes, Docker and REST APIs.

work.name: SESAR Lab, University of Milan
work.summary: Secure Service-oriented Architectures Research Lab (SESAR Lab)
highlights: Designed and implemented APIGuard Assurance, an API-agnostic, contract-driven tool for automated REST API security assurance based on a taxonomy of eight domains and specified oracles to establish trust in automated verdicts, validated against a real-world target.

work.name: SESAR Lab, University of Milan
highlights: Contributed to the design of the Assurance Engine, a Rust-based framework on Kubernetes.
Integrated Elasticsearch for data collection and querying.
Implemented REST APIs and formal contracts for verifying Non-Functional Properties (CIA triad, reliability, scalability).

skills.name: Soft Skills
keywords: Problems: address problems critically, solve problems/create solutions
Communication: cooperate with colleagues, work in teams, manage teamwork
Others: demonstrate curiosity, cope with stress, prioritise tasks

projects.name: APIGuard Assurance
projects.summary: Development of an API-agnostic, contract-driven tool for REST API security assurance, with a particular focus on semantic security violations that traditional functional and syntactic testing does not capture.
highlights: Built in Python 3.12, with a dynamic test-discovery mechanism allowing new security checks to be added without modifying the core engine.
Implemented specified oracles directly in each test's code: every check encodes its own evaluation criterion, derived from the domain knowledge of the control under test.
Enforced strict static typing (mypy) and runtime validation (Pydantic), with full documentation coverage across the public API.
Built reproducible packages with Hatch, verifying identical SHA-256 hashes across repeated builds.
Designed an extensible connector architecture to integrate external security scanners, decoupling test logic from tool-specific output parsing.

projects.name: FTP Client and Server
projects.summary: Developed a multi-threaded FTP-compliant client-server application in C for file transfer, fully interoperable with FileZilla.
highlights: Architecture: Multi-threaded server based on Socket Programming for concurrent session management.
Data Modes: Native support for both Active (PORT) and Passive (PASV) data transfer modes.
Protocol Management: Implemented essential FTP commands (e.g., USER, PASS, LIST, RETR, STOR, CWD...)
Key skills: C, socket programming, multithreading, network protocols, FTP, Git.

projects.name: Exhibition Web App
projects.summary: Built a dynamic web application serving as a digital portfolio for displaying multimedia content.
highlights: Developed the backend with Node.js/Express and the frontend with HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript/jQuery.
Integrated MongoDB for document-oriented storage and AJAX for asynchronous client-server communication (GET, POST, DELETE).
Managed source code and collaboration using Git and GitHub.
Key skills: Node.js, Express, MongoDB, AJAX, REST APIs, jQuery, Git.

projects.name: Data-Driven CV with LaTeX, Lua & GitHub Actions
projects.summary: Bilingual CV and cover letter generated from a single JSON source using LaTeX and Lua, with automated compilation and deployment via GitHub Actions.
highlights: Key Skills: LaTeX, Lua, GitHub Actions, CI/CD

projects.name: Event-Driven IoT Architecture (Kafka + Kong)
projects.summary: Development of a cloud-native microservices infrastructure on Kubernetes for IoT ingestion and monitoring, with a particular focus on non-functional properties such as scalability, fault tolerance, and edge-to-core security.
highlights: Microservices architecture based on Apache Kafka in KRaft mode (ZooKeeper-less) for asynchronous decoupling and reliable message handling (Zero Data Loss).
Integration of Kong API Gateway to implement the Gateway Offloading pattern, centralizing Authentication (API Key), Rate Limiting, and Load Balancing at the edge.
Storage layer optimized on MongoDB using Time Series Collections and hybrid compression (LZ4/Zstd) to maximize storage efficiency and analytical performance.
Full Kubernetes orchestration managed via Helm, featuring automatic scalability (HPA) and advanced security (Secrets, TLS/SASL) to meet non-functional requirements.
